reviews.review_msg: Such a bad professor. I don't recommend anyone to take his class. He literally assumed that no need to read students' post and never replied back. Besides, he pulled out analysis on pro and cons of two options but literally assume one option is correct and give low grade on the one that he doesn't think is correct

Doesn't follow text book, continuously says the textbook is wrong, this is right.  Made inappropriate comments about women in the workforce.  Has an ego that's hard to sit through.  Comments on grading doesn't make a lot of sense.
I really like this prof and he is actually not that difficult but it is a little sad that he didn't teach 225 again at uvic.

This course is an easy course. Clear grading rubric. i didn't put a lot of effort in this course. The prof is not that difficult but he is good at making simple things complicated. He is responsible and talks to students. but it is a lot of homework. if you follow what he says in the class, you are fine.

the course is manageable—formatting and econ content are easy, wating paper. But tough grader: one classmate chose a complex macro topic and trimmed less essential parts to fit time and suit a non-beginner audience. Still, the prof harshly cut marks for font size, lack of gestures, and "unclear" logic, despite a clear and well-structured talk.

Writing for economics course.

His feedback on an assignment: “This is a math question. Why so many words?”

Uhhh, maybe because it's a writing class?!?

Egotistical, stuck-up, and not fun to be around.

Lectures didn't cover important topics, Prepare to self study! TOUGH GRADER, assignments given were hard. If your new to econ, good luck! Discussions very important, high percentage. Just don't mess up. Practice given was kinda helpful . Professor Greg was intimidating, felt unwelcome in class.

He becomes frustrated at students when they ask questions. Avoid at all costs. In fact I am about the graduate and I can easily say the Greg is the worst prof I have seen throughout my undergraduate career.
The practice he provided was useful.
Gregory does not explain what is required. His info seems disorganized and he isn't great prof. Your best bet is to go to the ta's and get them on your good side so you understand what is expects.
He teaches as he hates you. Leaves everything to TA and doesn't even help when you personally ask him questions. Sending him emails and you will get very rude responses.

The most confusing prof I've ever known. He gives lectures but doesn't specify whether the lecture is enough or you'll have to review the textbook (sometimes this is not the case). Instead of studying, I would've to spend time review both materials to see if they are worth reviewing again. He wants students to study on their own but gives no help.

Doing preview is recommended. That's important for you to transfer to Beedie later. There will be lots you need to go through. You might feel painful as you attend his first lecture. You will feel better later on, because many students will choose not to show up in lectures, even exams without dropping the course. No worries, the course's curved.

His midterms are relatively easy, so pay attention to those. The final exam is a bit harder but still not that hard. He is a better prof for someone who can self-study rather than those who need lots of instructions.

He's the hardest proff I have seen in my whole life. He does not even answers your doubts and asks you a question when you go to him to ask a doubt. Never answers doubts directly. Very rude and seems not at all interested in teaching class.
Not only he gives ur grade harder but also your life harder

I dont know why but when students come to ask him questions in class , his attitude seems really disrespectful . And instead of giving answers he tries to ask the students back and if they cant answer him he will get mad
Do take him
He is not that horrible as some people said it. I had no problem with him Exams are fair also
He doesn't answer questions and expect students to have his brain. Will get mad when asking too many question

His Exams are easy. if you know had read his lecture notes well. then you can easily score more than B. Outside the class he is not that good. he want you to do your stuff yourself rather than getting help from him. His main idea is really good, but way of teaching is not good.
Newspaper article are the worst part. Don't choose him.
If u wanna pass econ class, do not take his. He is going tough way with easy class materials.
Don't choose him. He is the worst instructor ever. He can't explain anything.

If you want to get an A from Greg, you have to earn it. I took ECON103 with with  him (got A) & then took BUS207(economic for business) and got A+. Much of the marterial in this class is transferable and will be useful in other economic classes so the fact that he makes students work hard to earn their grades is actually useful in the long term.
IF YOU WANT TO PASS THIS COURSE, JUST DONT CHOOSE HIM 11!1!!1!!111!!
Just Don't .. All the study will have to be done by yourself.

The worst prof I have ever seen in my college year. He teaches less than 10% of the materials and you have to learn the rest of it by yourself. Provide answers for every in-class assignments without explanation. Yet, you will be bullied and mocked in front of the class if you ask him.
No words to describe this person.  No notes.  Worst instructor ever.

At first, I didnt like him at all. He seemed to be full of himsel and showed that he is a very tough professor. But honestly, he is a wonderful prof. His exams are  easy if you study your MCQs. All of the concepts he teaches are clear and concise. You will have to work for it, but deff worth it!

His classes are math inclined. He seems to be boasting all the time about him being the wonderful 1% gifted people in the world who are good at math.  Terrible at grading. The toughest econ103 class in SFU!  DO NOT take this course if YOU ARE BAD AT MATH!
Relatively hard lectures and hard exams. Practice all questions he provides (in-class assignments and online quiz) will give you good grades.
Best choice for Econ Average is pretty high though 80%+
Pretty good professor, clear and fair. exams are easy.
As his name suggests, he's Harder. Still, His concepts are clear, and the best part is the knowledge you gain could be used  in many other classes. =D
It seems like that he never forgive students, no late assignment is acceptable even if you are late about 0.000000001 second. IT IS LATE. The worst teacher I have ever seen.
Great teacher!!
Great teacher,pretty easy
Not very helpful or clear. Very smart and relevant however. If you learn better on your own with high textbook use go for it! If not.... :/
In my opinion poor at getting key terms across while lecturing. Slides are full of content but hardly chooses anything from slides demands high textbook use. I don't recommend MR. Harder
wicked prof

Pretty young prof - just finished his masters and this is his third course he's taught so far. Extremely good at explaining the material. So far I've barely used the text - lecture attendance is mandatory if you want to even pass this class. Definitely my favorite prof this term.
